tools.name: ICMPlugin_GetIncidentDetails
tools.description: Retrieves full incident details for a single ICM incident identified by its unique numeric incidentId. Use this tool when you already know the incident ID (e.g., from a prior search, message, or system reference) and need authoritative details such as status, severity, owners, timeline, and resolution

incidentId.description: Positive integer uniquely identifying the ICM incident (e.g., 1234567). Must not contain non-numeric characters or whitespace.

tools.name: ICMPlugin_GetSimilarIncidents
tools.description: Get similar incidents to the specified incident

incidentId.description: Positive integer uniquely identifying the ICM incident (e.g., 1234567) used to retrieve the similar incidents. Must not contain non-numeric characters or whitespace

tools.name: ICMPlugin_GetIncidentMitigationHints
tools.description: Get mitigation hints for the specified incident

incidentId.description: Positive integer uniquely identifying the ICM incident (e.g., 1234567) used to get mitigation hints, Must not contain non-numeric characters or whitespace

tools.name: ICMPlugin_GetIncidentContext
tools.description: Get incident context for the specified id

incidentId.description: Incident Id used to get context

tools.name: ICMPlugin_GetIncidentAISummary
tools.description: Get incident's AI generated summary for specified id

incidentId.description: Incident Id used to get AI generated summary

tools.name: IcmMcpIntegrationPlugin_GetIncidentBridgeSummary
tools.description: Get the on-call bridge conversation summary for the specified incident

incidentId.description: Incident Id used to get bridge conversation summary

tools.name: ICMDataPlugin_QueryIncidents
tools.description: Query IcM incidents based on the provided parameters from Athena.

showOutagesOnly.description: Whether to limit results to outages only

statusList.description: The statuses to include: ACTIVE, MITIGATED, RESOLVED

teamName.description: The IcM team name. Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

keywordList.description: Comma-separated keywords to search for in incident title and tags. Empty means no keyword filtering.

tools.name: WorkItemPlugin_QueryWorkItems
tools.description:
Asks questions about work items in Azure Devops like bugs, tasks, epics or features that can be answered by azure devops fields or the tags on the work item.

Follow-up questions should route back to this tool, when they are relevant to querying work items. Example:

User: What are the bugs assigned to me?
Assistant: <Response>
User: What are the active ones?

Where the question is NOT relevant to querying and therefore should NOT route back to this tool:

User: What are the bugs assigned to me including the severity?/Assistant: <Response>
User: What do the severity levels mean?

organization.description: The Azure DevOps organization. Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

project.description: The Azure DevOps project. Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

workItemType.description: The type of the work item the user is asking for or null if ambiguous

question.description: The user's question about work items including the work item type if the user includes it.

includeRelations.description: Whether to include related links like pull requests

tools.Name: WorkItemPlugin_CreateWorkItem
tools.Description: Creates an ADO work item (example: bug or task). Always call this tool for 'create a bug', 'create a task' or similar even if no parameters are provided

organization.description: The Azure DevOps organization. Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

project.description: The Azure DevOps project. Do NOT provide a value unless the user explicitly states it. Leave null — the system resolves this automatically.

type.description: Type of the work item to be created such as an Epic, Requirement, Feature, Task, Bug or User Story

title.description: Title of the ADO work item being created

description.description: Description of the ADO work item being created

areaPath.description: Area Path of the ADO work item being created

additionalFields.description: A map of additional fields to their values as a JSON map. Example: Create a bug with Status Tweet foo would produce {"Status Tweet": "foo"}

InputSchema.destructiveHint: This function performs an action that may modify data or state.

tools.name: wit_add_artifact_link
tools.description: Add artifact links (repository, branch, commit, builds) to work items. You can either provide the full vstfs URI or the individual components to build it automatically.

workItemId.description: The ID of the work item to add the artifact link to.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

artifactUri.description: The complete VSTFS URI of the artifact to link. If provided, individual component parameters are ignored.

projectId.description: The project ID (GUID) containing the artifact. Required for Git artifacts when artifactUri is not provided.

repositoryId.description: The repository ID (GUID) containing the artifact. Required for Git artifacts when artifactUri is not provided.

branchName.description: The branch name (e.g., 'main'). Required when linkType is 'Branch'.

commitId.description: The commit SHA hash. Required when linkType is 'Fixed in Commit'.

pullRequestId.description: The pull request ID. Required when linkType is 'Pull Request'.

buildId.description: The build ID. Required when linkType is 'Build', 'Found in build', or 'Integrated in build'.

linkType.description: Type of artifact link, defaults to 'Branch'. This determines both the link type and how to build the VSTFS URI from individual components.

comment.description: Comment to include with the artifact link.

tools.name: core_list_projects
tools.description: Retrieve a list of projects in your Azure DevOps organization.

stateFilter.description: Filter projects by their state. Defaults to 'wellFormed'

top.description: The maximum number of projects to return. Defaults to 100.

skip.description: The number of projects to skip for pagination. Defaults to 0.

continuationToken.description: Continuation token for pagination. Used to fetch the next set of results if available.

tools.name: repo_update_pull_request_reviewers
tools.description: Add or remove reviewers for an existing pull request.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request exists.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request to update.

reviewerIds.description: List of reviewer ids to add or remove from the pull request.

action.description: Action to perform on the reviewers. Can be 'add' or 'remove'.

tools.name: wit_get_work_item
tools.description: Get a single work item by ID.

id.description: The ID of the work item to retrieve.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

fields.description: Optional list of fields to include in the response. If not provided, all fields will be returned.

asOf.description: Optional date string to retrieve the work item as of a specific time. If not provided, the current state will be returned.

expand.description: Expand options include 'All', 'Fields', 'Links', 'None', and 'Relations'. Relations can be used to get child workitems. Defaults to 'None'.

tools.name: wiki_get_page
tools.description: Retrieve wiki page metadata by path. This tool does not return page content.

wikiIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of the wiki.

project.description: The project name or ID where the wiki is located.

path.description: The path of the wiki page (e.g., '/Home' or '/Documentation/Setup').

recursionLevel.description: Recursion level for subpages. 'None' returns only the specified page. 'OneLevel' includes direct children. 'Full' includes all descendants.

tools.name: wit_get_query
tools.description: Get a query by its ID or path.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

query.description: The ID or path of the query to retrieve.

expand.description: Optional expand parameter to include additional details in the response. Defaults to 'None'.

depth.description: Optional depth parameter to specify how deep to expand the query. Defaults to 0.

includeDeleted.description: Whether to include deleted items in the query results. Defaults to false.

useIsoDateFormat.description: Whether to use ISO date format in the response. Defaults to false.

tools.name: repo_list_pull_request_thread_comments
tools.description: Retrieve a list of comments in a pull request thread.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request is located.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request for which to retrieve thread comments.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread for which to retrieve comments.

project.description: Project ID or project name (optional)

top.description: The maximum number of comments to return.

skip.description: The number of comments to skip.

tools.name: core_list_project_teams
tools.description: Retrieve a list of teams for the specified Azure DevOps project.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

mine.description: If true, only return teams that the authenticated user is a member of. Defaults to true

top.description: The maximum number of teams to return. Defaults to 100.

skip.description: The number of teams to skip for pagination. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: work_list_iterations
tools.description: List all iterations in a specified Azure DevOps project.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

excludedIds.description: An optional array of iteration IDs, and their children, that should not be returned.

tools.name: work_get_team_capacity
tools.description: Get the team capacity of a specific team and iteration in a project.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team.

iterationId.description: The Iteration Id to get capacity for.

tools.name: wit_get_query_results_by_id
tools.description: Retrieve the results of a work item query given the query ID. Returns work item references.

id.description: The ID of the query to retrieve results for.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team. If not provided, the default team will be used.

timePrecision.description: Whether to include time precision in the results. Defaults to false.

top.description: The maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 50.

tools.name: wit_link_work_item_to_pull_request
tools.description: Link a single work item to an existing pull request.

projectId.description: The project ID of the Azure DevOps project (note: project name is not valid).

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository containing the pull request. Do not use the repository name here, use the ID instead.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request to link to.

workItemId.description: The ID of the work item to link to the pull request.

pullRequestProjectId.description: The project ID containing the pull request. If not provided, defaults to the work item's project ID (for same-project linking).

tools.name: wit_list_backlogs
tools.description: Receive a list of backlogs for a given project and team.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team.

tools.name: repo_get_branch_by_name
tools.description: Get a branch by its name.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the branch is located.

branchName.description: The name of the branch to retrieve, e.g., 'main' or 'feature-branch'.

tools.name: search_wiki
tools.description: Search Azure DevOps Wiki for a given search text

searchText.description: Keywords to search for wiki pages

project.description: Filter by projects

includeFacets.description: Include facets in the search results

top.description: Maximum number of results to return

tools.name: wit_get_work_item_type
tools.description: Get a specific work item type.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

workItemType.description: The name of the work item type to retrieve.

tools.name: repo_get_repo_by_name_or_id
tools.description: Get the repository by project and repository name or ID.

project.description: Project name or ID where the repository is located.

tools.name: wiki_list_wikis
tools.description: Retrieve a list of wikis for an organization or project.

project.description: The project name or ID to filter wikis. If not provided, all wikis in the organization will be returned.

tools.name: wiki_get_page_content
tools.description: Retrieve wiki page content by path.

wikiIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of the wiki.

project.description: The project name or ID where the wiki is located.

path.description: The path of the wiki page to retrieve content for (e.g., '/Home' or '/Documentation/Setup'). Defaults to root page if not provided.

tools.name: repo_list_pull_request_threads
tools.description: Retrieve a list of comment threads for a pull request.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request is located.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request for which to retrieve threads.

project.description: Project ID or project name (optional)

iteration.description: The iteration ID for which to retrieve threads. Optional, defaults to the latest iteration.

baseIteration.description: The base iteration ID for which to retrieve threads. Optional, defaults to the latest base iteration.

top.description: The maximum number of threads to return after filtering.

skip.description: The number of threads to skip after filtering.

status.description: Filter threads by status. If not specified, returns threads of all statuses.

authorEmail.description: Filter threads by the email of the thread author (first comment author).

authorDisplayName.description: Filter threads by the display name of the thread author (first comment author). Case-insensitive partial matching.

tools.name: wit_get_work_items_batch_by_ids
tools.description: Retrieve list of work items by IDs in batch.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

ids.description: The IDs of the work items to retrieve.

fields.description: Optional list of fields to include in the response. If not provided, a hardcoded default set of fields will be used.

tools.name: repo_list_branches_by_repo
tools.description: Retrieve a list of branches for a given repository.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the branches are located.

top.description: The maximum number of branches to return. Defaults to 100.

filterContains.description: Filter to find branches that contain this string in their name.

tools.name: repo_list_repos_by_project
tools.description: Retrieve a list of repositories for a given project

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

top.description: The maximum number of repositories to return.

skip.description: The number of repositories to skip. Defaults to 0.

repoNameFilter.description: Optional filter to search for repositories by name. If provided, only repositories with names containing this string will be returned.

tools.name: wit_work_items_link
tools.description: Link work items together.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

id.description: The ID of the work item to update.

linkToId.description: The ID of the work item to link to.

linkType.description: Type of link to create between the work items. Options include 'parent', 'child', 'duplicate', 'duplicate of', 'related', 'successor', 'predecessor', 'tested by', 'tests', 'affects', and 'affected by'. Defaults to 'related'.

comment.description: Optional comment to include with the link.

tools.name: repo_create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch in the repository.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the branch will be created.

branchName.description: The name of the new branch to create, e.g., 'feature-branch'.

sourceBranchName.description: The name of the source branch to create the new branch from. Defaults to 'main'.

sourceCommitId.description: The commit ID to create the branch from. If not provided, uses the latest commit of the source branch.

tools.name: search_workitem
tools.description: Get Azure DevOps Work Item search results for a given search text

searchText.description: Search text to find in work items

project.description: Filter by projects

includeFacets.description: Include facets in the search results

skip.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

continuationToken.description: Continuation token for pagination. Used to fetch the next set of results if available.

tools.name: repo_create_pull_request_thread
tools.description: Creates a new comment thread on a pull request.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request is located.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request where the comment thread exists.

content.description: The content of the comment to be added.

project.description: Project ID or project name (optional)

filePath.description: The path of the file where the comment thread will be created. (optional)

status.description: The status of the comment thread. Defaults to 'Active'.

rightFileStartLine.description: Position of first character of the thread's span in right file. The line number. Starts at 1. (optional)

rightFileStartOffset.description: Position of first character of the thread's span in right file. The character offset. Starts at 1. (optional)

rightFileEndLine.description: Position of last character of the thread's span in right file. The line number. Starts at 1. (optional)

rightFileEndOffset.description: Position of last character of the thread's span in right file. The character offset. (optional)

tools.name: wit_create_work_item
tools.description: Create a new work item in a specified project and work item type.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

workItemType.description: The type of work item to create, e.g., 'Task', 'Bug', etc.

fields.description: A record of field names and values to set on the new work item.

name.description: The name of the field, e.g., 'System.Title'.

tools.name: repo_get_pull_request_by_id
tools.description: Get a pull request by its ID.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request is located.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request to retrieve.

includeWorkItemRefs.description: Whether to reference work items associated with the pull request.

includeLabels.description: Whether to include a summary of labels in the response.

tools.name: work_list_team_iterations
tools.description: Retrieve a list of iterations for a specific team in a project.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team.

timeframe.description: The timeframe for which to retrieve iterations. Currently, only 'current' is supported.

tools.name: wit_add_child_work_items
tools.description: Create one or many child work items from a parent by work item type and parent id.

parentId.description: The ID of the parent work item to create a child work item under.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

workItemType.description: The type of the child work item to create.

items.description: Array of child work items to create.

title.description: The title of the child work item.

description.description: The description of the child work item.

areaPath.description: Optional area path for the child work item.

iterationPath.description: Optional iteration path for the child work item.

tools.name: wit_add_work_item_comment
tools.description: Add comment to a work item by ID.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

workItemId.description: The ID of the work item to add a comment to.

comment.description: The text of the comment to add to the work item.

tools.name: repo_list_pull_requests_by_commits
tools.description: Lists pull requests by commit IDs to find which pull requests contain specific commits

project.description: Project name or ID

commits.description: Array of commit IDs to query for

tools.name: wit_get_work_items_for_iteration
tools.description: Retrieve a list of work items for a specified iteration.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team. If not provided, the default team will be used.

iterationId.description: The ID of the iteration to retrieve work items for.

tools.name: wit_list_work_item_revisions
tools.description: Retrieve list of revisions for a work item by ID.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

workItemId.description: The ID of the work item to retrieve revisions for.

top.description: Optional number of revisions to retrieve. If not provided, all revisions will be returned.

skip.description: Optional number of revisions to skip for pagination. Defaults to 0.

expand.description: Optional expand parameter to include additional details. Defaults to 'None'.

tools.name: wit_list_work_item_comments
tools.description: Retrieve list of comments for a work item by ID.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

workItemId.description: The ID of the work item to retrieve comments for.

top.description: Optional number of comments to retrieve. Defaults to all comments.

tools.name: repo_update_pull_request_thread
tools.description: Updates an existing comment thread on a pull request.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request is located.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request where the comment thread exists.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread to update.

project.description: Project ID or project name (optional)

status.description: The new status for the comment thread.

tools.name: wiki_create_or_update_page
tools.description: Create or update a wiki page with content.

wikiIdentifier.description: The unique identifier or name of the wiki.

path.description: The path of the wiki page (e.g., '/Home' or '/Documentation/Setup').

content.description: The content of the wiki page in markdown format.

project.description: The project name or ID where the wiki is located.

etag.description: ETag for editing existing pages (optional, will be fetched if not provided).

branch.description: The branch name for the wiki repository. Defaults to 'wikiMaster' which is the default branch for Azure DevOps wikis.

tools.name: repo_list_my_branches_by_repo
tools.description: Retrieve a list of my branches for a given repository Id.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the branches are located.

top.description: The maximum number of branches to return.

filterContains.description: Filter to find branches that contain this string in their name.

tools.name: wit_my_work_items
tools.description: Retrieve a list of work items relevent to the authenticated user.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

type.description: The type of work items to retrieve.

top.description: The maximum number of work items to return. Defaults to 50.

includeCompleted.description: Whether to include completed work items. Defaults to false.

tools.name: wit_list_backlog_work_items
tools.description: Retrieve a list of backlogs of for a given project, team, and backlog category

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team.

backlogId.description: The ID of the backlog category to retrieve work items from.

tools.name: repo_reply_to_comment
tools.description: Replies to a specific comment on a pull request.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request is located.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request where the comment thread exists.

threadId.description: The ID of the thread to which the comment will be added.

content.description: The content of the comment to be added.

project.description: Project ID or project name (optional)

tools.name: wiki_get_wiki
tools.description: Get the wiki by wikiIdentifier.

wikiIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of the wiki.

project.description: The project name or ID where the wiki is located. If not provided, the default project will be used.

tools.name: work_assign_iterations
tools.description: Assign existing iterations to a specific team in a project.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

team.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps team.

iterations.description: An array of iterations to assign. Each iteration must have an identifier and a path.

identifier.description: The identifier of the iteration to assign.

path.description: The path of the iteration to assign, e.g., 'Project/Iteration'.

tools.name: wit_update_work_items_batch
tools.description: Update work items in batch.

updates.description: An array of updates to apply to work items. Each update should include the operation (op), work item ID (id), field path (path), and new value (value).

op.description: The operation to perform on the field.

id.description: The ID of the work item to update.

path.description: The path of the field to update, e.g., '/fields/System.Title'.

value.description: The new value for the field. This is required for 'Add' and 'Replace' operations, and should be omitted for 'Remove' operations.

format.description: The format of the field value. Only to be used for large text fields. e.g., 'Html', 'Markdown'. Optional, defaults to 'Html'.

tools.name: wit_update_work_item
tools.description: Update a work item by ID with specified fields.

id.description: The ID of the work item to update.

updates.description: An array of field updates to apply to the work item.

op.description: The operation to perform on the field.

path.description: The path of the field to update, e.g., '/fields/System.Title'.

value.description: The new value for the field. This is required for 'Add' and 'Replace' operations, and should be omitted for 'Remove' operations.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

tools.name: repo_search_commits
tools.description: Search for commits in a repository with comprehensive filtering capabilities. Supports searching by description/comment text, time range, author, committer, specific commit IDs, and more.

project.description: Project name or ID

version.description: The name of the branch, tag or commit to filter commits by

versionType.description: The meaning of the version parameter, e.g., branch, tag or commit

top.description: Maximum number of commits to return

searchText.description: Search text to filter commits by description/comment. Supports partial matching.

author.description: Filter commits by author email or display name

authorEmail.description: Filter commits by exact author email address

committer.description: Filter commits by committer email or display name

committerEmail.description: Filter commits by exact committer email address

fromDate.description: Filter commits from this date (ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')

toDate.description: Filter commits to this date (ISO 8601 format, e.g., '2024-12-31T23:59:59Z')

commitIds.description: Array of specific commit IDs to retrieve. When provided, other filters are ignored except top/skip.

tools.name: repo_create_pull_request
tools.description: Create a new pull request.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request will be created.

sourceRefName.description: The source branch name for the pull request, e.g., 'refs/heads/feature-branch'.

targetRefName.description: The target branch name for the pull request, e.g., 'refs/heads/main'.

title.description: The title of the pull request.

description.description: The description of the pull request. Must not be longer than 4000 characters. Optional.

isDraft.description: Indicates whether the pull request is a draft. Defaults to false.

workItems.description: Work item IDs to associate with the pull request, space-separated.

forkSourceRepositoryId.description: The ID of the fork repository that the pull request originates from. Optional, used when creating a pull request from a fork.

labels.description: Array of label names to add to the pull request after creation.

tools.name: wiki_list_pages
tools.description: Retrieve a list of wiki pages for a specific wiki and project.

wikiIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of the wiki.

project.description: The project name or ID where the wiki is located.

top.description: The maximum number of pages to return. Min value 1. Max value 100

continuationToken.description: Token for pagination to retrieve the next set of pages.

pageViewsForDays.description: Number of days to retrieve page views for. If not specified, page views are not included.

tools.name: wit_work_item_unlink
tools.description: Remove one or many links from a single work item

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

id.description: The ID of the work item to remove the links from.

linkType.description: Type of link to remove. Options include 'parent', 'child', 'duplicate', 'duplicate of', 'related', 'successor', 'predecessor', 'tested by', 'tests', 'affects', 'affected by', and 'artifact'. Defaults to 'related'.

url.description: Optional URL to match for the link to remove. If not provided, all links of the specified type will be removed.

tools.name: repo_list_pull_requests_by_repo_or_project
tools.description: Retrieve a list of pull requests for a given repository. Either repositoryId or project must be provided.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull requests are located.

project.description: The ID of the project where the pull requests are located.

top.description: The maximum number of pull requests to return.

skip.description: The number of pull requests to skip.

status.description: Filter pull requests by status. Defaults to 'Active'.

sourceRefName.description: Filter pull requests from this source branch (e.g., 'refs/heads/feature-branch').

targetRefName.description: Filter pull requests into this target branch (e.g., 'refs/heads/main').

tools.name: repo_update_pull_request
tools.description: Update a Pull Request by ID with specified fields, including setting autocomplete with various completion options.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository where the pull request exists.

pullRequestId.description: The ID of the pull request to update.

title.description: The new title for the pull request.

description.description: The new description for the pull request. Must not be longer than 4000 characters.

isDraft.description: Whether the pull request should be a draft.

status.description: The new status of the pull request. Can be 'Active' or 'Abandoned'.

autoComplete.description: Set the pull request to autocomplete when all requirements are met.

mergeStrategy.description: The merge strategy to use when the pull request autocompletes. Defaults to 'NoFastForward'.

deleteSourceBranch.description: Whether to delete the source branch when the pull request autocompletes. Defaults to false.

transitionWorkItems.description: Whether to transition associated work items to the next state when the pull request autocompletes. Defaults to true.

bypassReason.description: Reason for bypassing branch policies. When provided, branch policies will be automatically bypassed during autocompletion.

labels.description: Array of label names to replace existing labels on the pull request.

tools.name: work_create_iterations
tools.description: Create new iterations in a specified Azure DevOps project.

project.description: The name or ID of the Azure DevOps project.

iterations.description: An array of iterations to create. Each iteration must have a name and can optionally have start and finish dates in ISO format.

iterationName.description: The name of the iteration to create.

startDate.description: The start date of the iteration in ISO format (e.g., '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z'). Optional.

finishDate.description: The finish date of the iteration in ISO format (e.g., '2023-01-31T23:59:59Z'). Optional.

tools.name: get_portfolio
tools.title: Get portfolio
tools.description: Get detailed portfolio data by ID including name, owner, and projects. Use after finding portfolio ID via typeahead. Returns complete portfolio configuration. Essential for understanding portfolio context and content.

portfolio_gid.description: Globally unique identifier for the portfolio

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response

tools.name: get_portfolios
tools.title: Get portfolios
tools.description: List portfolios in workspace owned by the current user. REQUIRES workspace parameter. Returns portfolio names and IDs for portfolios you own. Use for portfolio discovery and management. Supports pagination for workspaces with many portfolios.

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response

tools.name: get_items_for_portfolio
tools.title: Get items for portfolio
tools.description: List projects, goals, and other items in a portfolio. Returns item names, IDs, and types. Use for portfolio content exploration and management. Supports pagination for portfolios with many items.

portfolio_gid.description: Globally unique identifier for the portfolio

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response

tools.name: get_project
tools.title: Get project
tools.description: Get detailed project data including name, description, owner, members, custom fields, and settings. Use after finding project ID via typeahead. Returns complete project configuration needed for task operations. Specify opt_fields for custom fields data.

project_id.description: Globally unique identifier for the project.

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response.

tools.name: get_projects
tools.title: Get projects
tools.description: List projects filtered by workspace. Supports team, archived filters. Returns project names and IDs. Use for filtered project views and bulk operations.

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response.

limit.description: Number of projects to return per page (1-100).

tools.name: create_project
tools.title: Create project
tools.description: Create new project in Asana. Every project requires a workspace, and this cannot be changed after creation. If workspace is an organization, a team must also be specified. Client agents should explicitly ask for team when creating a project rather than inferring it. Supports custom fields, templates, due dates, and privacy settings. Returns project ID for immediate task creation. Essential for project setup.

team.description: Globally unique identifier for the team. Required if workspace is not provided.

color.description: Color of the project (light-green, dark-green, light-blue, dark-blue, etc).

due_date.description: Date on which this project is due (YYYY-MM-DD format).

start_date.description: Date on which this project is started (YYYY-MM-DD format).

is_template.description: Whether the project is a template. Defaults to false.

public.description: True if project is public to the entire workspace. Defaults to false.

default_view.description: The default view of the project (list, board, calendar, timeline).

custom_fields.description: JSON string of custom fields in format: {"field_gid": "value"}.

owner.description: User identifier for project owner. Can be "me", an email, or a user GID.

followers.description: Comma-separated list of user GIDs to add as followers.

members.description: Comma-separated list of user GIDs to add as members.

default_access_level.description: Default access level for project members (admin, editor, commenter, viewer).

minimum_access_level_for_customization.description: Minimum access level for customization (admin, editor).

minimum_access_level_for_sharing.description: Minimum access level for sharing (admin, editor, commenter, viewer).

privacy_setting.description: Privacy level for the project (public_to_workspace, private_to_team, private).

archived.description: Whether the project is archived. Defaults to false.

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in response.

tools.name: get_status_overview
tools.title: Get status overview
tools.description: Get status overview and progress reports for initiatives/projects. Use this tool as a standalone when users ask for: status updates, status reports, project status, work overview, progress overview, initiative status, identified blockers, or any status-related queries. This tool searches projects and portfolios, extracts tasks and status updates, and returns aggregated project details, tasks, and status information. IMPORTANT: Call this tool directly with keywords - do NOT call other search tools first. This tool handles all searching and aggregation internally.

keywords.description: Keywords to search for projects. Can be a single keyword (e.g., 'asana') or multiple comma-separated keywords (e.g., 'asana, chatgpt, integration'). The tool searches each keyword separately and combines results from all matching projects (OR logic - matches any keyword).

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in task data (e.g., 'name,notes,assignee,due_on,completed').

tools.name: search_tasks
tools.title: Search tasks
tools.description: Advanced task search with multiple filters. Supports text search, all date filters, status filters, user filters, project/portfolio/section/tag filters, and custom fields. Returns matching tasks with IDs. Handles complex queries and bulk task operations. When the user asks about tasks assigned to them, use assignee_any="me" for best results. Note: If you specify projects_any and sections_any, you will receive tasks for the project and tasks for the section. If you're looking for only tasks in a section, omit the projects_any from the request.

text.description: Text to search for in task name or description.

resource_subtype.description: Filters results by the task's resource_subtype (e.g., milestone).

completed.description: Filter for completed or incomplete tasks.

is_blocked.description: Filter to tasks with incomplete dependencies.

is_blocking.description: Filter to incomplete tasks with dependents.

assignee_not.description: Comma-separated list of user identifiers to exclude.

followers_not.description: Comma-separated list of user identifiers to exclude.

created_by_not.description: Comma-separated list of user identifiers to exclude.

assigned_by_not.description: Comma-separated list of user identifiers to exclude.

liked_by_not.description: Comma-separated list of user identifiers to exclude.

commented_on_by_not.description: Comma-separated list of user identifiers to exclude.

projects_not.description: Comma-separated list of project IDs to exclude.

projects_all.description: Comma-separated list of project IDs (tasks must be in all).

sections_any.description: Comma-separated list of section or column IDs.

sections_not.description: Comma-separated list of section or column IDs to exclude.

sections_all.description: Comma-separated list of section or column IDs (tasks must be in all).

tags_not.description: Comma-separated list of tag IDs to exclude.

tags_all.description: Comma-separated list of tag IDs (tasks must have all).

due_on_before.description: ISO 8601 date string for due date before filter.

due_on_after.description: ISO 8601 date string for due date after filter.

due_on.description: ISO 8601 date string or null for due date.

due_at_before.description: ISO 8601 datetime string for due datetime before filter.

due_at_after.description: ISO 8601 datetime string for due datetime after filter.

start_on_before.description: ISO 8601 date string for start date before filter.

start_on_after.description: ISO 8601 date string for start date after filter.

start_on.description: ISO 8601 date string or null for start date.

created_on_before.description: ISO 8601 date string for creation date before filter.

created_on_after.description: ISO 8601 date string for creation date after filter.

created_on.description: ISO 8601 date string or null for creation date.

created_at_before.description: ISO 8601 datetime string for creation datetime before filter.

created_at_after.description: ISO 8601 datetime string for creation datetime after filter.

completed_on_before.description: ISO 8601 date string for completion date before filter.

completed_on_after.description: ISO 8601 date string for completion date after filter.

completed_on.description: ISO 8601 date string or null for completion date.

completed_at_before.description: ISO 8601 datetime string for completion datetime before filter.

completed_at_after.description: ISO 8601 datetime string for completion datetime after filter.

modified_on_before.description: ISO 8601 date string for modified date before filter.

modified_on_after.description: ISO 8601 date string for modified date after filter.

modified_on.description: ISO 8601 date string or null for modified date.

modified_at_before.description: ISO 8601 datetime string for modified datetime before filter.

modified_at_after.description: ISO 8601 datetime string for modified datetime after filter.

sort_by.description: Field to sort by (e.g., 'due_date', 'created_at', 'completed_at', 'likes', 'modified_at'). Defaults to modified_at.

sort_ascending.description: Sort in ascending order. Defaults to false.

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response.

opt_pretty.description: Provides "pretty" output with line breaking and indentation.

custom_fields.description: JSON string of custom field filters in format: {"field_gid": "value"}.

tools.name: get_task
tools.title: Get task
tools.description: Get full task details by ID. Returns name, description, assignee, due dates, custom fields, projects, dependencies. Essential before updating tasks. Use opt_fields for custom field values. Required for understanding task context.

task_id.description: Globally unique identifier for the task.

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response.

tools.name: create_task
tools.title: Create task
tools.description: Create task in Asana with context. REQUIRES one of: project_id, parent, or workspace+assignee together. For assignee-only tasks, both workspace and assignee must be provided. Returns task ID with confirmation. You can directly tag and mention people by using links in html_notes field

project_id.description: Project GID for task creation. Required if not using parent or workspace+assignee.

parent.description: Parent task GID to create this as a subtask.

html_notes.description: Task description in HTML format. You can create links to other asana objects with the following instructions: For tags specifically, to make it easier to create @-mentions through the API, we only require that you provide the GID of the object you wish to reference. If you have access to that object, the API will automatically generate the appropriate href and other attributes for you. For example, to create a link to a task with GID "123", you can send the tag which will then be expanded to Task Name. You can also generate a link to a task in a specific project or tag by including a data-asana-project or data-asana-tag attribute in the tag. All other attributes, as well as the contents of the tag, are ignored. To keep the contents of your tag and make a custom vanity link, include the property data-asana-dynamic="false" when setting the contents of the tag. You would send This is some custom text! and receive This is some custom text! If you do not have access to the referenced object when you try to create a link, the API will not generate an href for you, but will instead look for an href you provide. This allows you to write back tags unmodified even if you do not have access to the resource. If you do not have access to the referenced object and no href is provided, your request will be rejected with a 400 Bad Request error. Similarly, if you provide neither a GID nor a valid href, the request will be rejected with the same error.

assignee.description: User identifier for task assignment. Can be "me", an email, or a user GID. When used without project_id, workspace is also required.

due_at.description: Due date and time in ISO 8601 format.

completed.description: True if the task is initially marked complete.

section_id.description: Section GID to place task in. Requires project_id to be provided.

assignee_section.description: Section GID to place task in the assignee's My Tasks list.

followers.description: Comma-separated list of user identifiers. Can be "me", email addresses, or user GIDs.

start_at.description: Start date and time in ISO 8601 format.

custom_fields.description: JSON string of custom fields in format: {"field_gid": "value"}.

custom_type.description: GID of a custom type to apply to the task.

custom_type_status_option.description: GID of a custom type status option to apply to the task.

tools.name: update_task
tools.title: Update task
tools.description: Update existing task properties. Change name, notes, assignee, completion status, due dates, custom fields. Requires task ID. Returns updated task data. Use asana_get_task first to understand current state.

task_id.description: Globally unique identifier for the task to update.

html_notes.description: New task description in HTML format. You can create links to other asana objects with the following instructions: For tags specifically, to make it easier to create @-mentions through the API, we only require that you provide the GID of the object you wish to reference. If you have access to that object, the API will automatically generate the appropriate href and other attributes for you. For example, to create a link to a task with GID "123", you can send the tag which will then be expanded to Task Name. You can also generate a link to a task in a specific project or tag by including a data-asana-project or data-asana-tag attribute in the tag. All other attributes, as well as the contents of the tag, are ignored. To keep the contents of your tag and make a custom vanity link, include the property data-asana-dynamic="false" when setting the contents of the tag. You would send This is some custom text! and receive This is some custom text! If you do not have access to the referenced object when you try to create a link, the API will not generate an href for you, but will instead look for an href you provide. This allows you to write back tags unmodified even if you do not have access to the resource. If you do not have access to the referenced object and no href is provided, your request will be rejected with a 400 Bad Request error. Similarly, if you provide neither a GID nor a valid href, the request will be rejected with the same error.

assignee.description: User identifier to assign the task to. Can be "me", an email, or a user GID.

due_at.description: Due date and time in ISO 8601 format.

custom_fields.description: JSON string of custom fields in format: {"field_gid": "value"}.

tools.name: get_tasks
tools.title: Get tasks
tools.description: List tasks filtered by context (workspace/project/tag/section/user list). One context required. Supports assignee, date filters. Returns task names and IDs. Use for filtered task views and bulk operations.

project.description: Globally unique identifier for the project.

tag.description: The tag GID to retrieve tasks for

section.description: Globally unique identifier for the section.

user_task_list.description: Globally unique identifier for the user task list.

assignee.description: User identifier to assign the task to. Can be "me", an email, or a user GID.

completed_since.description: Filter for tasks completed since this date in datetime format.

modified_since.description: Filter for tasks modified since this date in datetime format.

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response.

limit.description: Number of sections to return per page (1-100).

tools.name: search_objects
tools.title: Search objects
tools.description: Quick search across Asana objects. ALWAYS use this FIRST before specialized search. Returns most relevant items based on recency and usage. Faster than dedicated search tools for finding specific items. If the user is not specific about which objects to search, use the following priority order: project + task > portfolio > goal + others (user, team, tag, custom_field).

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response.

resource_type.description: The type of resource to search for.

query.description: The search query. Can be empty to get default results for the resource type.

count.description: Number of results to return. Default is 20 if parameter is empty. Minimum 1, maximum 100.

tools.name: get_user
tools.title: Get user
tools.description: Get user details by ID, email, or "me". Returns name, email, workspaces. Use to find user IDs for task assignment. "me" returns authenticated user info. Essential before assigning tasks. When no user_id is provided, defaults to "me" (authenticated user) - equivalent to the former asana_get_user_info tool.

user_id.description: A string identifying a user. This can be 'me', an email, or the gid of a user. When omitted, defaults to 'me' (authenticated user).

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response.

tools.name: get_workspace_users
tools.title: Get workspace users
tools.description: Get all users in workspace. Returns compact users with names and IDs. Results are sorted alphabetically and limited to 2000. Prefer searching more narrowly, like by typeahead or team first instead of this.

opt_fields.description: Comma-separated list of optional fields to include in the response.

offset.description: Pagination offset token from a previous request.

tools.name: atlassianUserInfo
tools.description: Get current user info from Atlassian

tools.name: getAccessibleAtlassianResources
tools.description: Get cloudid to construct API calls to Atlassian REST APIs

tools.name: getConfluenceSpaces
tools.description: Get spaces from Confluence. Spaces are containers for pages and content, organized by teams or topics. Use this to discover available spaces before accessing their content. You can also use this with a space key to get the numerical space ID needed for other operations.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

ids.description: Filter by specific numerical space IDs

favoritedBy.description: Filter spaces favorited by a specific user account ID

notFavoritedBy.description: Filter spaces not favorited by a specific user account ID

includeIcon.description: Include space icon information in response

cursor.description: Pagination cursor for retrieving next page of results

limit.description: Maximum number of spaces to return (default: 25, max: 250)

tools.name: getConfluencePage
tools.description: Get a specific page or live doc data (including body content) from Confluence by its ID. Returns the page body content converted to Markdown format. You can extract the page ID from Confluence URLs - for example, from a URL like https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SPACE/pages/123456789/Page+Title, the page ID is "123456789". The URL can be used as the cloudId parameter, as the tool will handle converting the site name to a cloud ID.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

pageId.description: The unique identifier of the page to retrieve. Can be extracted from Confluence URLs (e.g., the numeric ID in the URL path)

tools.name: getPagesInConfluenceSpace
tools.description: Get all pages within a specific Confluence space. Useful for discovering content structure and finding pages by title or status. Returns page body content as Markdown.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

spaceId.description: The unique numerical identifier of the space to search within

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to return (default: 25, max: 250)

depth.description: Filter pages by depth, default: all

subtype.description: Filter pages by subtype. Use "live" for live docs or "page" for regular pages

tools.name: createConfluencePage
tools.description: Create a new page in Confluence. Can create regular pages or live docs based on the subtype parameter.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

spaceId.description: The unique numerical identifier of the space where the page will be created

title.description: The title of the new page

parentId.description: The unique identifier of the parent page (if creating a child page)

body.description: The content of the page. You **MUST** use markdown format.

isPrivate.description: The page will be private. Only the user who creates this page will have permission to view and edit one.

subtype.description: The subtype of the page. Set to "live" to create a live doc, or omit to create a regular page.

tools.name: updateConfluencePage
tools.description: Update an existing page or Live Doc in Confluence.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

pageId.description: The unique identifier of the existing page to update

title.description: The new title for the page (if not provided, retains the existing title)

status.description: The status of the page to update (defaults to current). If draft is specified, then the page's draft version will be updated.

spaceId.description: The unique numerical identifier of the space (if moving the page)

parentId.description: The unique identifier of the parent page (if changing hierarchy)

body.description: The content of the page. You **MUST** use markdown format.

versionMessage.description: Optional message describing the changes made in this version

tools.name: createConfluenceFooterComment
tools.description: Create a footer comment on a Confluence page or blog post. Footer comments are general comments about the page that appear at the bottom.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

pageId.description: The id of the page to add the comment to.

parentCommentId.description: The id of the parent comment to reply to.

attachmentId.description: The id of the attachment to add to the comment.

customContentId.description: The id of the custom content to add to the comment.

body.description: The content of the comment in Markdown format.

tools.name: createConfluenceInlineComment
tools.description: Create an inline comment on a page or blog post. Inline comments are attached to specific text selections within the page content.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

pageId.description: The id of the page to add the comment to.

parentCommentId.description: The id of the parent comment to reply to.

body.description: The content of the comment in Markdown format.

textSelectionMatchCount.description: The number of matches for the selected text on the page (should be strictly greater than textSelectionMatchIndex)

textSelectionMatchIndex.description: The match index to highlight. This is zero-based. E.g. if you have 3 occurrences of "hello world" on a page and you want to highlight the second occurrence, you should pass 1 for textSelectionMatchIndex and 3 for textSelectionMatchCount.

inlineCommentProperties.description: Object describing the text to highlight on the page/blog post. Only applicable for top level inline comments (not replies) and required in that case.

tools.name: searchConfluenceUsingCql
tools.description: Search content in Confluence using CQL (Confluence Query Language). CQL is a powerful query language for finding pages, spaces, and content based on various criteria like title, content, labels, creation date, etc.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

cql.description: The CQL query string (e.g., "title ~ 'meeting' AND type = page")

cqlcontext.description: The context for the CQL query execution

cursor.description: Pagination cursor for retrieving next page of results

expand.description: Comma-separated list of properties to expand in the response

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 25, max: 250)

prev.description: Include previous page link in pagination

next.description: Include next page link in pagination

tools.name: getJiraIssue
tools.description: Get the details of a Jira issue by issue id or key.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

issueIdOrKey.description:
Issue id or key can be used to uniquely identify an existing issue.
Issue id is a numerical identifier. An example issue id is 10000.
Issue key is formatted as a project key followed by a hyphen '-' character and then followed by a sequential number.
An example issue key is ISSUE-1.

tools.name: editJiraIssue
tools.description: Update the details of an existing Jira issue id or key.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

issueIdOrKey.description:
Issue id or key can be used to uniquely identify an existing issue.
Issue id is a numerical identifier. An example issue id is 10000.
Issue key is formatted as a project key followed by a hyphen '-' character and then followed by a sequential number.
An example issue key is ISSUE-1.

tools.name: createJiraIssue
tools.description: Create a new Jira issue in a given project with a given issue type.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

projectKey.description:
A project key in Jira is a unique identifier (a string of letters, numbers and sometimes underscores) of a project.
      There is a tool "getVisibleJiraProjects" to look up which projects the user has create permission to create a new Jira issue.

issueTypeName.description:
A Jira issue type categorizes and  distinguishes different kinds of work being tracked within a project.
      It's a way to define what a specific piece of work represents.
      For example, in a Jira Software project, there are "Epic", "Story", "Task", "Bug" or "Subtask" issue types by default.
      Or in Jira Service Management, there are "Change", "IT help", "Incident", "New feature", "Problem", "Service request", "Service request with approval" or "Support" issue types by default.
      User can remove those default ones and/or define their own issue types as well.
      There is a tool "getJiraProjectIssueTypesMetadata" to get the available issue types in a given project.

description.description: The content of the issue's description in Markdown format.

parent.description: The issue key or id of the parent issue for "Subtask" issue types.

assignee_account_id.description:
During issue creation, we can set an assignee for the new issue.
      The input must be an account id of a valid user in the given cloud id.
      There is a tool "atlassianUserInfo" to get the account id of the current user.
      There is a tool "lookupJiraAccountId" to get the account ids of the existing users in Jira based on the user's display name or email address.

tools.name: getTransitionsForJiraIssue
tools.description: Get available transitions for an existing Jira issue id or key.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

issueIdOrKey.description:
Issue id or key can be used to uniquely identify an existing issue.
Issue id is a numerical identifier. An example issue id is 10000.
Issue key is formatted as a project key followed by a hyphen '-' character and then followed by a sequential number.
An example issue key is ISSUE-1.

tools.name: transitionJiraIssue
tools.description: Transition an existing Jira issue (that has issue id or key) to a new status.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

issueIdOrKey.description:
Issue id or key can be used to uniquely identify an existing issue.
Issue id is a numerical identifier. An example issue id is 10000.
Issue key is formatted as a project key followed by a hyphen '-' character and then followed by a sequential number.
An example issue key is ISSUE-1.

additionalProperties.description: This is the field value. The actual value will depends on the field type.

tools.name: lookupJiraAccountId
tools.description: Lookup account ids of existing users in Jira based on the user's display name or email address.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

tools.name: searchJiraIssuesUsingJql
tools.description: Search Jira issues using Jira Query Language (JQL).

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

jql.description: A Jira Query Language (JQL) expression to search Jira issues

maxResults.description: A maximum number of issue to search per page. Default is 50, max is 100

nextPageToken.description: This is used for pagination purpose to fetch more data if a JQL search has more issues in next pages

tools.name: addCommentToJiraIssue
tools.description: Adds a comment to an existing Jira issue id or key.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

issueIdOrKey.description:
Issue id or key can be used to uniquely identify an existing issue.
Issue id is a numerical identifier. An example issue id is 10000.
Issue key is formatted as a project key followed by a hyphen '-' character and then followed by a sequential number.
An example issue key is ISSUE-1.

commentBody.description: The content of the comment in Markdown format.

type.description: Whether visibility of this comment is restricted to a group or role.

value.description: The name of the group or role that visibility of this comment is restricted to.

tools.name: getJiraIssueRemoteIssueLinks
tools.description: Get remote issue links (eg: Confluence links etc...) of an existing Jira issue id or key

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

issueIdOrKey.description:
Issue id or key can be used to uniquely identify an existing issue.
Issue id is a numerical identifier. An example issue id is 10000.
Issue key is formatted as a project key followed by a hyphen '-' character and then followed by a sequential number.
An example issue key is ISSUE-1.

globalId.description:
An identifier for the remote item in the remote system.
          For example, the global ID for a remote item in Confluence would consist of the app ID and page ID, like this: appId=456&pageId=123.
          When a global ID is provided, this tool returns only the remote issue link of the given Jira issue that has the provided global ID.
          When no global ID is provided, this tool returns all the remote issue links of the given Jira issue.

tools.name: getVisibleJiraProjects
tools.description: Get visible Jira projects for which the user has either view, browse, edit or create permission on that project.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

searchString.description: Filter the results using a literal string. Projects with a matching key or name are returned (case insensitive)

action.description:
Filter results by projects for which the user can:
      * view the project
      * browse the project
      * edit the project
      * create the project, meaning that they have the Create issues project permission for the project in which the issue is created.

startAt.description: The index of the first item to return in a page of results (page offset).

maxResults.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.

expandIssueTypes.description: To include additional information about the issue types associated with the project

tools.name: getJiraProjectIssueTypesMetadata
tools.description: Get a page of issue type metadata for a specified project. The issue type metadata will be used to create issue.

cloudId.description: Unique identifier for an Atlassian Cloud instance in the form of a UUID. Can also be a site URL or extracted from Atlassian URLs - for example, from https://yoursite.atlassian.net/wiki/.... If not working, use the 'getAccessibleAtlassianResources' tool to find accessible Cloud IDs.

startAt.description: The index of the first item to return in a page of results (page offset).

maxResults.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.

tools.name: search_code

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub's powerful code search syntax. Examples: 'content:Skill language:Java org:github', 'NOT is:archived language:Python OR language:go', 'repo:github/github-mcp-server'. Supports exact matching, language filters, path filters, and more.

tools.name: get_commit
tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository.

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository.

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

tools.name: get_discussion_comments

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_discussions
tools.description: List discussions for a repository.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

category.description: Optional filter by discussion category ID. If provided, only discussions with this category are listed.

orderBy.description: Order discussions by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

repo.description: Repository name. If not provided, discussions will be queried at the organisation level.

tools.name: add_issue_comment

tools.name: list_issues

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: search_issues
tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories.

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: get_notification_details
tools.description: Get detailed information for a specific GitHub notification.

tools.name: list_notifications
tools.description: Lists all GitHub notifications for the authenticated user.

before.description: Only show notifications updated before the given time (ISO 8601 format)

filter.description: Filter notifications to, use default unless specified. Read notifications are ones that have already been acknowledged by the user. Participating notifications are those that the user is directly involved in, such as issues or pull requests they have commented on or created.

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only notifications for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only notifications for this repository are listed.

since.description: Only show notifications updated after the given time (ISO 8601 format)

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: merge_pull_request
tools.description: Merge a pull request in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: update_pull_request
tools.description: Update an existing pull request in a GitHub repository.

draft.description: Mark pull request as draft (true) or ready for review (false)

reviewers.description: GitHub usernames to request reviews from

tools.name: update_pull_request_branch
tools.description: Update the branch of a pull request with the latest changes from the base branch.

expectedHeadSha.description: The expected SHA of the pull request's HEAD ref

tools.name: add_comment_to_pending_review
tools.description: Add review comment to the requester's latest pending pull request review.

body.description: The text of the review comment

line.description: The line of the blob in the pull request diff that the comment applies to. For multi-line comments, the last line of the range

path.description: The relative path to the file that necessitates a comment

side.description: The side of the diff to comment on. LEFT indicates the previous state, RIGHT indicates the new state

startLine.description: For multi-line comments, the first line of the range that the comment applies to

startSide.description: For multi-line comments, the starting side of the diff that the comment applies to. LEFT indicates the previous state, RIGHT indicates the new state

subjectType.description: The level at which the comment is targeted

annotations.title: Add review comment to the requester's latest pending pull request review

tools.description: Submit the requester's latest pending pull request review.

body.description: The text of the review comment

annotations.title: Submit the requester's latest pending pull request review

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.
